channel.title: IngestThis
channel.description: Articles, tutorials, and resources for Data Engineers, Scientists, Analysts, and Architects.

item.title: High-Performance Columnar Transfers: Combining Apache Arrow Flight and Iceberg REST Catalogs
item.description: Modern lakehouse architecture is easier to reason about when you separate two questions. The first question is how a system discovers and governs a ta

item.title: Block vs. Object Storage: A Deep Dive Into the Foundation of Modern Data, and How the Lakehouse Made the Slow Option Fast
item.description: Deep dive into block vs object storage, how Apache Parquet, Apache Iceberg, and query engines turned object storage into the foundation of interactive analytics.

item.title: The Buyer's Scorecard for Agentic Analytics: Evaluating Tooling in the Enterprise AI Era
item.description: Agentic analytics demos are easy to enjoy and hard to evaluate. A user asks a question, an assistant answers, a chart appears, and the room leans forw

item.title: Building Closed-Loop Decision Agents: Moving from Passive BI Dashboards to Active Goal-Directed Workflows
item.description: Dashboards are excellent at showing people what happened. They are less good at deciding what should happen next.

item.title: Conversational AI on Managed Iceberg: Exposing Amazon S3 Tables through MCP
item.description: The most interesting part of conversational analytics is not the chat box. The chat box is just the surface area. The harder question is what happens

item.title: Decoupled Catalogs vs. Managed Tables: Architectural Freedom in the Age of Table Format Convergence
item.description: Open table formats have changed buyer expectations. A few years ago, the question was whether an organization should put more analytical data into obj

item.title: Preparing Your Data Lakehouse for the EU AI Act: Auditable Lineage and Data Provenance
item.description: The EU AI Act changes the conversation around AI architecture because it makes trust operational. It is not enough to say that an AI system is useful,

item.title: Federation and the Lakehouse: Two Roads to Unified Data Access, and How to Know Which One to Take
item.description: How federation and the lakehouse unify data access, with a decision framework, lifecycle pattern, and best practices for using both together.

item.title: Enforcing Fine-Grained Security at Machine Speed: Dynamic Access Control for High-Frequency AI Agents
item.description: AI agents change the security model for analytics. A human user may run a handful of queries, pause, interpret the answer, and ask a follow-up. An age

item.title: Delete Files vs Deletion Vectors in Apache Iceberg: How V3 Rewrote the Economics of Changing Data
item.description: Iceberg v2 delete files vs v3 deletion vectors. How Roaring bitmaps and per-file vectors transformed merge-on-read performance for CDC and streaming workloads.

item.title: The Variant Type in Apache Iceberg: How Shredding Turns Messy JSON Into Fast Analytics
item.description: Apache Iceberg v3 introduces the Variant type for flexible JSON storage with columnar performance. Learn how shredding enables fast analytics on semi-structured data.

item.title: The State of Apache Iceberg v4 in July 2026: What the Dev List Tells Us About the Format's Next Chapter
item.description: What the Iceberg v4 dev list tells us about adaptive metadata trees, single-file commits, column updates, and the format's next chapter in mid-2026.

item.title: Designing Idempotent Pipelines in the Agentic Lakehouse: Eliminating Double-Write Anomalies
item.description: Agents retry. Networks fail. Jobs time out after doing some work. APIs return ambiguous responses. Schedulers run the same workflow twice. A human cli

item.title: The 2026-07-28 Model Context Protocol Release Candidate: What the Stateless Spec Means for Data Platforms
item.description: The date in this topic matters. Today is July 6, 2026. A release candidate dated July 28, 2026 is still in the future. That means this article should

item.title: The Metric Contract Mandate: Standardizing Semantic Layers Before AI Agent Access
item.description: AI agents are very good at moving quickly. That is the opportunity and the risk. If an agent can inspect metadata, generate queries, compare results,

item.title: Multi-Engine Catalog Federation with Apache Polaris: Syncing Google Cloud, AWS, and Azure Metadata
item.description: Open table formats changed the data lakehouse conversation, but they did not finish it. A table can be stored in an open format and still be hard to g

item.title: Personal Context vs. Shared Context: A Deep Dive Into How Humans and Organizations Should Feed Their AI Agents
item.description: How humans and organizations should engineer AI agent context - personal memory vs governed shared knowledge, with decision frameworks.

item.title: Designing Private, Air-Gapped Data Lakehouses: Scaling Iceberg in Highly Secure, On-Premises Clouds
item.description: Some of the most important lakehouse work happens in environments that will never look like a simple public-cloud reference architecture. Defense, pub

item.title: Migrating Proprietary Warehouses to Open Lakehouses: The 2026 Playbook for Zero-Copy Metadata Translation
item.description: Every warehouse migration sounds simpler before the first inventory. Then the team discovers old dashboards, hidden dependencies, undocumented stored

item.title: The State of Agentic AI Standards in 2026: MCP, A2A, WebMCP, OSI, and the Protocol Stack Taking Shape
item.description: The agentic AI protocol stack is solidifying in 2026 — MCP for tools, A2A for agents, WebMCP for the web, OSI for semantics, payments, identity, and security.
